name: antenna/patch
description: Probe-fed rectangular patch on a finite grounded slab: real Result.antenna pipeline over the NF2FF box; matched-broadside TM010 is a documented gap.
reference: cavity model TM010 (f_r=3.391 GHz), broadside D >= 5 dBi
target: pipeline valid (6 NF2FF faces, p_rad>0, closure<0.05); broadside D>=5 dBi matched (GAP)
tolerance_basis: Two-part: (1) PIPELINE (pass) -- Result.antenna runs end to end over the grounded slab, returns 6 air-exterior NF2FF faces per frequency, p_rad>0, and a best radiated-vs-accepted closure < 0.05. (2) PHYSICS (gap) -- the probe on this thick finite-ground slab is reactance-dominated (best |S11| ~ 0 dB) and radiates off-broadside, so the matched-broadside TM010 D >= 5 dBi target is not met and is a documented gap (strict xfail in the e2e test).
falsification: EXECUTED (tests/rf/antenna/test_antenna_benchmark_e2e.py): the pipeline gate is a real PASS; the physical matched-broadside gate genuinely xfails (broadside D max -6.97 dBi < 5 and best match -0.0 dB > -10), so its strict xfail is exercised, not vacuous.

metrics.note: matched-broadside TM010 gate target; documented GAP on this thick finite-ground slab

notes: Pipeline valid=True (faces=[6, 6, 6, 6, 6], closure_min=0.0050); broadside D max -6.97 dBi, best match -0.0 dB, cavity f_r ~ 3.391 GHz (outside the 4.4-6.0 GHz run band). The matched-broadside TM010 physics + the external reference-solver cross-check remain open (feed/ground redesign); reference is pending-generation (no lumped-feed adapter mapping).
